hero-tagline: I build stable, scalable automation systems that solve real business problems. From architecture to deployment.
about-text: I am a person who finds it important to understand how and why everything works. I am interested in systems: technical, logical, and human. I dislike chaos and random decisions, so I break down any idea into its structure and bring it to a working, stable state.
about-app-3: A system, not a collection of unrelated functions
about-tg-1: Bots as part of a complete system
about-val-3: Thoughtful style in code and interface
flow-1-desc: Deep understanding of the goal and requirements.
flow-2-desc: Designing the logic, database, and system interactions.
flow-3-desc: Writing clean, scalable, and maintainable code.
flow-4-desc: Ensuring stability and handling edge cases.
proj-desc-1: Automated contest management system with admin panel and analytics.
cont-note-text: You can also contact me directly:
hero-support: I design interfaces, automation flows, and Telegram-based products that are clear, reliable, and ready to grow.
specialty-1-text: Responsive pages, dashboards, landing layouts, and polished interactions in pure HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.
specialty-2-text: Bots, scripts, event logic, admin tools, and backend flows that reduce manual work and keep processes predictable.
specialty-3-text: I do not stop at code. I structure the flow, clarify the task, and shape the final interface around a real use case.
featured-text: This portfolio is strongest when it shows complete systems: visual interface, business logic, and real user flow.
cont-cta-title: Need a landing page, dashboard, bot, or automation flow?
cont-cta-text: I am easiest to reach by email. If you want, you can also check the code and project history on GitHub first.
pa-tagline: Integrated Telegram account management system: website admin panel, automated bot, access control and analytics in one product.
pa-problem-text: The hard part of this project was not sending commands to Telegram, but coordinating many accounts, permissions, admin visibility, and automation rules without turning the system into chaos or exposing sensitive account data.
pa-solution-text: I built a comprehensive system consisting of a Telegram bot for automated actions, a web-based admin dashboard for management, and a secure database. The system ensures safe participation in giveaways while providing real-time analytics.
pa-feat-1: Role System: Granular access control for admins and users.
pa-feat-2: Access Control: Secure login and action authorization.
pa-feat-3: Logging: Detailed logs of all bot and admin actions.
pa-feat-4: Automation Logic: Smart scripts for joining giveaways.
pa-feat-5: Monitoring: Real-time status of all connected accounts.
pa-concept-text: The main goal of the system is to automate account participation in giveaways, ensure user anonymity, and provide the administrator with full control through a web interface. The architecture is modular and scalable: new features can be added without rewriting the entire codebase.
pa-admin-li1: Create users with ID, name and username.
pa-admin-li2: Assign roles, restrictions and access levels.
pa-admin-li3: Link one or multiple accounts to a user.
pa-user-li1: Bot access is granted only after activation via the website.
pa-user-li2: Manage multiple accounts under one ID.
pa-user-li3: View statistics: participation, success rate, activity.
pa-user-li4: Secure workflow with codes and passwords.
pa-bot-li1: Automatically participates in giveaways: subscriptions, captchas, scripted actions.
pa-bot-li2: Tracks account activity and displays statistics to the user.
pa-bot-li3: Synchronized with the website via database: panel changes instantly affect the bot.
pa-bot-li4: Asynchronous processing allows managing multiple accounts simultaneously.
pa-bot-li5: Only active and authorized accounts participate in campaigns.
pa-db-struct-p: Users, accounts, statistics and logs. State is preserved after restart, analytics and export available.
pa-db-manage-p: Add, edit and delete users/accounts, control bot actions and permissions.
pa-db-ui-p: Tables, cards, filtering, activity visualization, hover effects and responsive navigation.
pa-sec-li1: Personal data is hidden and accessible only to the administrator.
pa-sec-li2: Account access is protected by codes and passwords.
pa-sec-li3: Bot and administrator actions are logged without exposing private information.
pa-sec-li4: Modular architecture reduces risks of errors and vulnerabilities.
pa-adv-li1: Centralized management of users and accounts.
pa-adv-li2: Automation of giveaway participation without routine manual tasks.
pa-adv-li3: Detailed analytics for accounts and users.
pa-adv-li5: Easy scalability and integration of new modules.
pa-gal-cap1: Admin dashboard: access, roles, action control.
pa-gal-cap2: User journey: Telegram interaction and participation scenarios.
pa-gal-cap3: Infrastructure: stability, data storage and scalability.
pa-res-text: This is not just a bot, but a full-fledged system: every action is under control, every account is protected, and the administrator sees the complete picture in real time. The solution is suitable for scalable automation, modular development and transparent Telegram account management.
proj-desc-2: Telegram-first AI userbot with control bot, memory, reminders, live data, and natural-language actions.
aiu-tagline: A Telegram-first AI system that works from the owner's own account, adds a dedicated control bot, supports an optional public chat bot, and keeps memory, reminders, live data, and automation inside one flow.
aiu-problem-text: Most Telegram AI projects are thin wrappers around a single model call. They can answer messages, but they do not behave like a real operating layer for an account: weak memory, no action routing, limited control, and too much friction between chat, tools, and automation.
aiu-solution-text: The project combines a Pyrogram userbot, a separate control bot, an optional public chat bot, layered memory, reminder scheduling, live-data tools, and structured Telegram action routing. The result is a compact but capable AI operating system built directly around Telegram.
aiu-stat-1-text: Dialogue, Telegram actions, and AI tasks are split into dedicated command channels.
aiu-stat-2-text: Runtime state is stored in local JSON files and Telegram session files for portability.
aiu-stat-3-text: No separate frontend is required for the owner to manage reminders, replies, and actions.
aiu-stat-4-text: AI models can be switched or backed off when limits, failures, or edge cases appear.
aiu-arch-card-1-text: The main engine runs from the owner's Telegram account via Pyrogram and handles direct commands, chat context, auto-replies, reminders, and action execution.
aiu-arch-card-2-text: A separate Telegram bot provides a control layer for runtime settings, response modes, allow/block lists, fallback behavior, and feature toggles.
aiu-arch-card-3-text: When enabled, a public-facing bot exposes the AI to normal chat users without replacing the owner-centric userbot flow.
aiu-arch-flow-text: The project avoids a classic web stack and keeps the interaction loop inside Telegram. Instead of browser forms and dashboards, the system routes natural language into concrete actions, memory updates, and live requests.
aiu-mode-d-text: Dialogue and owner workflow mode. It handles planning, reminders, memory updates, directives, everyday assistance, and longer-form requests.
aiu-mode-k-text: Structured Telegram action mode. This is where the system turns natural language into concrete chat operations: forward, delete, archive, inspect, and move content between chats.
aiu-mode-b-text: AI task mode for summarization, search, live information, image reasoning, and voice transcription through Groq-backed services.
aiu-memory-card-1-text: The system stores owner profile data, user-specific memory, entity facts, shared short-term context, and style signals. This creates continuity between chats without turning the project into a heavyweight platform.
aiu-memory-card-2-text: Style weighting lets the assistant balance the owner's voice, the target user's tone, and current chat context. That makes replies feel less generic and much more aligned with how the owner actually communicates.
aiu-memory-li1: Owner knowledge is stored separately and can be rewritten without touching code.
aiu-memory-li2: Per-user profiles and entity memory accumulate context over time.
aiu-memory-li3: Reminder and scheduler state survives restarts.
aiu-memory-li4: JSON persistence keeps deployment simple and transparent.
aiu-live-card-1-text: Search routing combines DuckDuckGo, Google, Wikipedia, GitHub search, and optional SearXNG, then extracts article content for better grounded answers.
aiu-live-card-2-text: Weather, exchange rates, news, and general live requests are treated as first-class tools, not as afterthoughts glued onto a chat bot.
aiu-live-card-3-text: Voice transcription and image analysis expand the assistant beyond plain text and make it useful in actual Telegram usage patterns.
aiu-ops-card-1-text: The project is driven by .env, session files, JSON state, and owner knowledge content. That keeps setup explicit: Telegram credentials, Groq API key, command behavior, search toggles, and memory options.
aiu-ops-card-2-text: The design includes outgoing-only command restrictions, identity limits, validation, silence logic, and safety filters so automation stays useful without turning reckless.
aiu-ops-li1: No SQL database, Redis, or Docker is required for the core workflow.
aiu-ops-li2: Pyrogram session files are sensitive and must remain private.
aiu-ops-li3: The system is designed to run as a persistent Python process on a local machine or VPS.
aiu-ops-li4: The control bot is operationally important because it exposes runtime management without a web admin.
aiu-stack-2-text: Userbot transport and Telegram session handling.
aiu-stack-3-text: LLM, vision, and voice transcription services.
aiu-stack-4-text: Concurrent task handling, scheduling, and chat workflows.
aiu-stack-5-text: Network requests for live tools and retrieval features.
aiu-stack-6-text: Portable runtime state without a separate database layer.
aiu-result-text: Dekrov AI Userbot stands out because it treats Telegram as the primary product surface, not as a thin integration. It combines assistant behavior, structured actions, memory, reminders, live retrieval, and model management into one coherent toolchain. As a portfolio case, it shows strong systems thinking: the project is opinionated, modular, practical, and clearly built around real usage instead of demo-only AI features.
don-tagline: This page exists for people who want to back the work, help cover infrastructure costs, and keep the project moving.
don-card-1-text: Dekrov.dev is not just a static showcase. It is part portfolio, part live product surface, and part experimentation space for bots, automations, interfaces, and AI workflows.
don-card-1-text-2: Support helps keep the infrastructure online and gives me more room to continue building, refining, and shipping new things.
don-list-1: Server costs for hosting and backend experiments.
don-list-2: Domain and basic infrastructure needed to keep the site online.
don-list-3: Ongoing iteration on portfolio pages, automations, and Telegram-based tools.
don-metric-3-label: Support methods will start with crypto
don-methods-text: The first support options on this page will be crypto-based. The visual structure is ready, and the exact wallets can be added cleanly next.
don-method-btc-text: Prepared for a direct wallet address.
don-method-eth-text: Ready for an on-chain support option.
don-method-ton-text: A good fit for Telegram-native support flows.
don-method-usdt-text: Prepared as a stable support option.
don-note: This page is here to keep support options in one place. Clear, direct, and without extra ceremony.
back-to-Giveaway-Bot-&-Admin-Dashboard: Back to Giveaway Bot & Admin Dashboard

python.tech-page-title: Python: A Fundamental Breakdown of the Ecosystem
python.tech-main-desc: Python is a high-level language that combines incredible readability with powerful low-level capabilities. Its architecture is built around the CPython virtual machine, which executes compiled bytecode, making it an ideal tool for automation and backend development.
python.tech-card-1-text: Analysis of the virtual machine operation, bytecode interpretation, and stack-based architecture.
python.tech-card-2-text: In-depth memory management through reference counting and a heuristic garbage collector.
python.tech-card-3-text: A cooperative multitasking model based on asyncio for high-load systems.
python.full-sec-1-text: When you run a script, Python first translates it into bytecode—a set of low-level instructions. The CPython virtual machine executes them sequentially, ensuring cross-platform compatibility. However, unlike Java, Python interprets bytecode dynamically, providing the flexibility to change variable types at runtime. This allows you to write concise code that can be easily scaled for any business task.
python.full-sec-2-title: 2. Memory Management and the GIL Problem
python.full-sec-2-text: In Python, the primary memory cleanup mechanism is Reference Counting: an object is deleted as soon as no more references point to it. For cyclic references, a Garbage Collector is used. A crucial limitation is the Global Interpreter Lock (GIL). This mutex protects data thread-safety but restricts parallel execution of bytecode across different threads. To circumvent this in heavy tasks, we use the multiprocessing module or asynchrony.
python.full-sec-project-case-text: As I already mentioned in the main section of my portfolio, Python is my key tool. In this project, I used it to build the bot's modular architecture. To guarantee data purity, I applied the **Pydantic** library for strict validation of incoming objects. All the giveaway business logic was moved into separate asynchronous services, which made it possible to isolate the Telegram API from heavy computations. Also, for in-depth system state monitoring, the **Loguru** library was implemented, providing detailed logging of every automation step.
python.project-ai-userbot-case-text: In Dekrov AI Userbot, Python is the orchestration layer for command parsing, memory, reminders, action routing, chat-bot support, and owner workflows. The value here is not just language familiarity but the ability to keep one Telegram-native system modular while it coordinates AI, automation, and runtime state in one process.
python.project-visitor-ai-case-text: In Visitor AI Assistant, Python holds together the public consultation flow: session lifecycle, moderation counters, drift detection, request drafting, and owner handoff all live in the same runtime. It is the layer that keeps the assistant helpful and structured instead of turning into a loose generic chatbot.

aiogram.tech-main-desc: Aiogram is a fully asynchronous framework built on top of asyncio. It encourages developers to write clean, typed, and modular code using modern web development patterns.
aiogram.tech-card-1-text: A multi-level system of filters and routers for managing data flows.
aiogram.tech-card-2-text: Implementation of user paths by persisting states in Redis or RAM.
aiogram.tech-card-3-text: Intermediate processing layers for implementing cross-cutting logic (logging, anti-spam).
aiogram.full-sec-1-text: In Aiogram, each event (Update) passes through a chain of Middlewares. This allows you to inject logic before the message reaches the main handler. For example, you can automatically check if a user is subscribed to a channel or limit request frequency (Rate limiting). This makes the system protected against abuse and allows you to move common logic into separate layers, without cluttering the command processing code.
aiogram.full-sec-2-text: To create complex interfaces, a Finite State Machine is used. It remembers which stage of the dialogue the user is at. To prevent data loss during a bot restart, we connect external storages (Redis/Postgres). This is the standard for Enterprise solutions, allowing you to seamlessly update the bot code without interrupting active user sessions.
aiogram.full-sec-project-case-text: As I already mentioned on the main page of my portfolio, I specialize in developing Telegram systems. In this project, I used **Aiogram 3.x** to build an asynchronous interaction architecture. I implemented a **Custom Middlewares** system to automatically check user subscription to sponsor channels before allowing entry to the giveaway. A state machine (**FSM**) with a **Redis** storage was also implemented, allowing administrators to configure giveaways step-by-step (uploading media, selecting a date, setting conditions) without losing progress even during a server reboot. To protect against cheating, I integrated custom filters on the dispatcher side that cut off suspicious bot activity.
aiogram.project-visitor-ai-case-text: Aiogram is the transport layer of the visitor assistant: consultation start buttons, callbacks, moderation actions, and public chat routing are all built around bot-first interaction patterns. That makes it a strong fit for a visitor product that must feel responsive while still preserving clear boundaries.

postgres.tech-main-desc: PostgreSQL is an object-relational DBMS known for its reliability, support for complex queries, and strict adherence to ACID standards.
postgres.tech-card-1-text: Multi-Version Concurrency Control allows reading data without locks during writes.
postgres.tech-card-2-text: Guarantee of data integrity even during sudden power or system failures.
postgres.tech-card-3-text: Efficient handling of unstructured data within SQL tables.
postgres.full-sec-1-text: Postgres guarantees that each operation will either be fully completed or not at all (atomicity). The Write-Ahead Logging (WAL) mechanism records all changes to a log before they are applied to the table. This is critically important for financial or gaming systems where an error in a single record could lead to a loss of consistency across the entire database.
postgres.full-sec-2-text: We use not only standard B-tree indexes but also GIN indexes to speed up searches in JSONB fields. This allows Postgres to combine the speed of NoSQL solutions with the reliability of classical relational databases. Proper query planning (Explain Analyze) helps optimize load and ensure instant response even on million-row selections.
postgres.full-sec-project-case-text: As I already mentioned on the main page, I use databases to ensure maximum system stability. In this project, I chose PostgreSQL for its extensibility and reliability. To integrate with the bot's asynchronous code, I used **SQLAlchemy** together with the **asyncpg** driver, which allowed the database to work without blocking the main thread. I designed the relational schema so that even with 100,000+ participants, winner selection happens instantly. This was achieved by setting up composite **B-tree indexes** on user ID and giveaway ID fields, guaranteeing 100% data consistency during mass operations.

flask.tech-main-desc: Micro-frameworks for creating server-side logic and APIs. Flask is used for flexible admin panels, and FastAPI for high-performance asynchronous services with automatic data validation.
flask.tech-card-1-text: Architecture without boilerplate—connect only the modules you need for each task.
flask.tech-card-2-text: In FastAPI, data is automatically validated at the type level, eliminating request errors.
flask.tech-card-3-text: Automatic interactive API documentation is available 'out of the box'.
flask.full-sec-1-text: When designing the backend, I adhere to REST principles. Each resource corresponds to its own URL, and actions are defined by standard HTTP methods. FastAPI implements a powerful dependency injection (DI) system. This makes it easy to manage database connections, authentication, and settings, making the code modular and suitable for automatic testing. All data exchange occurs in JSON format, ensuring ideal compatibility with any frontend frameworks.
flask.full-sec-2-text: For Flask, we use Jinja2—a secure templating engine. It allows for dynamic HTML generation on the server by inserting data from Python directly into the markup. In the case of FastAPI, fully asynchronous I/O is used. This means the server doesn't 'sleep' during a long database request but continues processing other incoming calls. This is critically important for monitoring dashboards where data must be updated instantly.

git.tech-main-desc: Git is a distributed version control system that is the industry standard. It allows you to save a history of changes, work in a team, and safely experiment with code.
git.tech-card-1-text: Creating isolated branches for each new feature (Feature-driven development).
git.tech-card-2-text: Git stores complete snapshots of the project state, not file differences, for reliability.
git.tech-card-3-text: The ability to break changes into logical parts for easy rollback and analysis.
git.full-sec-1-text: Git is essentially a graph database. Each commit has a unique SHA-1 hash, which guarantees that the code content hasn't been altered unnoticed. Unlike older systems (SVN), Git stores complete file snapshots. This makes operations like switching between branches (checkout) and merging instant. Understanding concepts like the 'index' (staging area) and 'tree' allows me to efficiently manage even the most complex merge conflicts.
git.full-sec-2-text: For stable development, I use Git Flow. The main branch always contains only tested, production-ready code. Development occurs in the 'develop' branch, and each new feature is created in a separate 'feature/' branch. This prevents unfinished code from breaking the working system. Using commands like cherry-pick and rebase allows me to maintain a clean and understandable commit history, which is critical for long-term project maintenance.

frontend.tech-page-title: HTML / CSS / JS: The Visual Interface
frontend.tech-main-desc: The trio of technologies that shape the user experience. We use modern markup, styling, and scripting standards to create fast and responsive web applications.
frontend.tech-card-1-text: Layout using Flexbox and Grid for perfect display on all device types.
frontend.tech-card-2-text: Using asynchrony and modules in JS for smooth interface operation.
frontend.tech-card-3-text: Focus on intuitiveness, accessibility, and modern visual effects (Glassmorphism).
frontend.full-sec-1-text: Modern frontend is not just static pages. With JavaScript, I manipulate the DOM (Document Object Model) tree, dynamically changing the interface in response to user actions. Using the Fetch API allows for background data exchange with the backend. This creates the effect of a Single Page Application (SPA), where the page does not fully reload but only smoothly updates the necessary data blocks, significantly enhancing user comfort.
frontend.full-sec-2-text: For styling, I use CSS variables and modern positioning methods. This makes it easy to implement dark and light themes. Animations are implemented using hardware acceleration (transform: translate3d), guaranteeing smooth 60 FPS even on mobile devices. Special attention is paid to the layout methodology: I separate styles into logical modules so they can be easily reused in different parts of the project.
frontend.full-sec-project-case-text: As I already mentioned on the main page of my portfolio, I pay great attention not only to the code but also to the user's visual comfort. In this project, I implemented a responsive admin panel in pure **JavaScript** without using heavy frameworks, which ensured instant loading. I applied modern layout techniques (Flexbox and CSS Grid) to create a complex 'glass' interface (Glassmorphism) that remains readable on any device. All communication with the backend occurs through asynchronous **Fetch API** requests, allowing the administrator to manage giveaways and track participant statistics without a single page reload.

asyncio.tech-main-desc: Asyncio is a library to write concurrent code using the async/await syntax. It is the foundation for modern high-load systems handling thousands of network connections simultaneously.
asyncio.tech-card-1-text: The core loop that manages the execution of all tasks in a single thread.
asyncio.tech-card-2-text: Functions that can pause their work without blocking the entire program.
asyncio.tech-card-3-text: The system doesn't wait for network responses, switching to other tasks instantly.
asyncio.full-sec-1-text: Unlike classic threads, Asyncio uses a model where coroutines yield control back to the Event Loop. This eliminates race conditions for shared data and allows a single process to serve a colossal number of requests, consuming ten times less RAM. This makes Python ideal for microservices and bots.
asyncio.full-sec-2-text: Asyncio operates on low-level system calls (epoll/kqueue), allowing it to monitor thousands of sockets. The program 'sleeps' at the OS kernel level until data arrives. This ensures that one request's latency doesn't affect the overall throughput of the entire system.
asyncio.full-sec-project-case-text: **I didn't mention Asyncio on the main page**, but in this project, it was a key technology. I used asynchrony to implement **multi-accounting** — the bot can work with different Telegram sessions simultaneously without interference. Using `asyncio.gather()`, I configured parallel subscription checks in 5-10 channels at once. This reduced the user's waiting time from several seconds to milliseconds. Asynchronous queues (Tasks) also ensured stable background mailings that don't slow down the main bot interface during active giveaways.
asyncio.project-ai-userbot-case-text: Asyncio is the architectural backbone of Dekrov AI Userbot: reminders, live requests, background jobs, auto-replies, and command execution all coexist in one persistent runtime. The project uses asynchrony as a system design choice, not as a small implementation detail.
asyncio.project-visitor-ai-case-text: Visitor AI Assistant relies on asyncio for concurrent message handling, session updates, cooldowns, and moderation checks. That lets the public assistant stay responsive while still maintaining state and background logic in parallel.

aiu-meta-description: Dekrov AI Userbot: a Telegram-first AI system with userbot logic, control bot, memory, reminders, live data, and automation.
aiu-open-text: Full source code, setup details, and documentation are available on GitHub.
aiu-open-li1: Telegram userbot and control bot source code
aiu-open-li2: Memory, reminders, live tools, and AI routing logic
aiu-open-li3: Project structure, configuration, and setup instructions
proj-desc-3: Public-facing Telegram AI assistant for consultations, soft routing, session memory, moderation, and owner handoff.
vis-meta-description: Visitor AI Assistant: a public-facing Telegram consultation layer with session memory, moderation, routing, and owner handoff.
vis-tagline: A public-facing Telegram AI assistant built for first-contact conversations, project discovery, request drafting, moderation, and smooth handoff to the owner.
vis-private-text: This project is presented as its own public product layer inside the broader Dekrov AI ecosystem. It is designed for consultations and routing, not for owner-only internals.
vis-private-li1: Built for first contact, not generic endless chat.
vis-private-li2: Keeps temporary session context while the consultation is active.
vis-private-li3: Can escalate meaningful requests to the owner without exposing private system behavior.
vis-problem-text: A public AI assistant has to do more than answer politely. If it is too generic, it wastes leads; if it is too open, it leaks internal context; if it is too rigid, it kills useful conversations. The challenge is building a first-contact layer that helps people move forward without becoming either a dumb FAQ or an unrestricted chatbot.
vis-solution-text: Visitor AI Assistant was built as a separate consultation contour: it answers public questions, helps visitors shape a meaningful request, preserves short session context, and keeps clear behavioral boundaries.
vis-stat-1-text: Conversation context is kept only for the active consultation and expires after inactivity.
vis-stat-2-text: The assistant stays inside public-facing knowledge and avoids owner-only internals.
vis-stat-3-text: Useful requests can be turned into a cleaner message for the owner instead of forcing visitors to start cold.
vis-stat-4-text: It can detect drift, keep boundaries, and end low-signal conversations when the dialogue stops being useful.
vis-arch-card-1-text: Visitors talk to a Telegram bot that starts and manages a consultation session instead of exposing the owner-facing userbot directly.
vis-arch-card-2-text: The assistant classifies requests, answers public questions, helps formulate messages, and routes useful conversations toward an owner handoff path.
vis-arch-card-3-text: Session state, moderation, boundary tracking, and quality review keep the visitor layer useful without letting it turn into an unrestricted generic chatbot.
vis-features-card-1-text: Instead of just dropping links, the assistant can help visitors understand what they need, clarify a task, and prepare a message before contacting the owner.
vis-features-card-2-text: When the conversation becomes meaningful, the visitor flow can redirect the user toward a clearer owner request rather than leaving them with a vague chat log.
vis-features-li1: Short-lived session memory preserves the active consultation without growing into a heavy long-term profile system.
vis-features-li2: Supportive drafting helps users who do not know how to describe a bot, automation flow, or collaboration request yet.
vis-features-li3: Small talk is allowed in a limited form, but the assistant keeps its role as an assistant instead of pretending to be a friend.
vis-features-li4: Public answers are separated from deeper owner-only project knowledge so the visitor layer stays clean and safe.
vis-ops-card-1-text: The flow can warn, limit, block, or end a consultation when behavior becomes abusive, repetitive, or clearly detached from the assistant's purpose.
vis-ops-card-2-text: Active consultation data is temporary by design: a session can expire after inactivity, and forced restarts do not keep unlimited conversational baggage.
vis-ops-li1: The system distinguishes meaningful follow-up questions from low-signal drift or empty chatter.
vis-ops-li2: Owner handoff stays explicit, so the assistant does not pretend it already completed something that still needs human review.
vis-ops-li3: A separate review layer can flag poor visitor replies and repeated degradation patterns for later fixing.
vis-ops-li4: The visitor contour remains separate from the owner-side assistant logic, which helps preserve safer public behavior.
vis-stack-1-text: Core logic, orchestration, moderation, and session handling.
vis-stack-2-text: Telegram bot transport, callbacks, routing, and visitor-facing interface handling.
vis-stack-3-text: LLM inference for consultation replies, drafting, and review logic.
vis-stack-4-text: Async workflows for message handling, session updates, and background checks.
vis-stack-5-text: Session state, moderation state, and lightweight runtime storage without a heavy separate backend.
vis-result-text: Visitor AI Assistant turns the first contact layer into an actual product surface: helpful enough to guide people, strict enough to keep direction, and structured enough to pass meaningful requests forward. As a portfolio case, it shows product thinking around boundaries, public UX, moderation, and AI behavior quality, not just raw chatbot generation.

pyrogram.tech-page-title: Pyrogram: Telegram client automation in Python
pyrogram.tech-main-desc: Pyrogram is a Python MTProto client that lets applications work with Telegram accounts and bots directly. It is useful when a project needs session-based control, message handling, media operations, and lower-level Telegram features in one library.
pyrogram.tech-card-1-text: Pyrogram manages authenticated Telegram sessions, reconnects, and account state without forcing the project to implement the MTProto protocol manually.
pyrogram.tech-card-2-text: Handlers, filters, dialogs, and media APIs make it possible to build userbot flows that react to chats, commands, and forwarding scenarios.
pyrogram.tech-card-3-text: Because it works with real Telegram accounts, Pyrogram is suited for automation, moderation, utilities, and owner-driven workflows.
pyrogram.full-sec-1-text: Pyrogram is especially useful when the product is not just a public bot but a system that acts through a real Telegram account. In this mode, the application can read dialogs, send messages, inspect media, and coordinate actions in ways that the regular Bot API does not expose. That is why it fits userbot architecture well.
pyrogram.full-sec-2-text: A practical strength of Pyrogram is the combination of session management with ergonomic chat APIs. The developer gets access to messages, users, files, and chat metadata through one consistent interface. That reduces glue code and lets the system focus on routing, safety, and business logic instead of protocol plumbing.
pyrogram.project-ai-userbot-case-text: Pyrogram is what makes the userbot side possible. Dekrov AI Userbot works through the owner's real Telegram account, so dialog access, media handling, cross-chat actions, and account-level automation all depend on a client library that exposes Telegram beyond the regular Bot API.

groq.tech-page-title: Groq API: Fast LLM inference for assistant workflows
groq.tech-main-desc: Groq API provides low-latency model inference for text, reasoning, vision, and transcription-oriented assistant tasks. It is valuable when an AI system needs quick turnarounds inside active chat workflows.
groq.tech-card-1-text: Fast responses matter in conversational products, especially when AI is part of an interactive chat loop instead of an offline batch process.
groq.tech-card-2-text: The API exposes different model capabilities that can be routed for dialogue, summarization, multimodal requests, and fallback behavior.
groq.tech-card-3-text: Groq-backed responses can be combined with memory, retrieval, and execution logic to create a more capable assistant layer.
groq.full-sec-1-text: When a Telegram assistant is expected to reply naturally and stay useful in live conversation, latency becomes a product concern, not just an infrastructure metric. A faster inference path makes the assistant feel more immediate, keeps command workflows practical, and reduces friction when the user is switching between dialogue, actions, and retrieval.
groq.full-sec-2-text: Modern assistant systems rarely rely on one simple text prompt. They route between summarization, search synthesis, image understanding, and voice transcription. An API layer like Groq becomes part of the orchestration strategy: choosing models, handling limits, and preserving a stable experience when one path needs fallback logic.
groq.project-ai-userbot-case-text: Groq is the inference layer behind dialogue, .b tasks, transcription, vision, and model fallback inside the userbot ecosystem. In this project, speed matters because the assistant is part of a live Telegram workflow where answers, tools, and action planning need to stay responsive.
groq.project-visitor-ai-case-text: In the visitor layer, Groq powers consultation replies, request drafting, and quality review of public-facing answers. Here the goal is not just raw generation, but fast and stable guidance during the first contact with a visitor.

httpx.tech-page-title: HTTPX: Async network layer for live tools
httpx.tech-main-desc: HTTPX is a modern Python HTTP client with both sync and async interfaces. It is well suited for projects that depend on external APIs, live retrieval, timeouts, retries, and structured request handling.
httpx.tech-card-1-text: The asynchronous client integrates naturally with asyncio applications and allows many outbound requests to be handled without blocking the main flow.
httpx.tech-card-2-text: Timeouts, headers, authentication, payload formats, and response parsing can be managed in a clean and explicit way.
httpx.tech-card-3-text: HTTPX works well as the transport layer for weather, news, rates, search backends, and other live-data integrations.
httpx.full-sec-1-text: In an assistant project, external requests should not freeze the rest of the system. HTTPX fits naturally into async applications, where one part of the app can wait for a remote API while other tasks continue running. Reusing clients also helps keep network behavior more efficient and predictable under load.
httpx.full-sec-2-text: A reliable HTTP layer is about more than sending GET requests. The application needs careful timeout strategy, validation, and clear handling of failed responses. That is especially important when live tools affect what the assistant says or does, because network instability should degrade gracefully instead of breaking the whole workflow.
httpx.project-ai-userbot-case-text: HTTPX is the network layer for live tools, retrieval, and grounded answers inside Dekrov AI Userbot. Search, rates, weather, article fetching, and other external lookups all depend on a client that behaves predictably inside an async assistant pipeline.

json-persistence.tech-page-title: JSON Persistence: Portable runtime state without a database
json-persistence.tech-main-desc: JSON persistence is a lightweight way to store structured runtime data without introducing a separate database server. It works well when the project values portability, transparency, and simple deployment.
json-persistence.tech-card-1-text: JSON files are human-readable, easy to inspect, and simple to back up, which is useful during development and operations.
json-persistence.tech-card-2-text: A project can move between machines or VPS environments with fewer infrastructure requirements when its state is file-based.
json-persistence.tech-card-3-text: Even without a database, structured files still require careful conventions for validation, updates, and data ownership.
json-persistence.full-sec-1-title: 1. Why JSON works at this scale
json-persistence.full-sec-1-text: Not every automation product needs SQL, Redis, or a heavy persistence layer on day one. If the runtime state is moderate, the ownership model is clear, and the deployment target is controlled, JSON files can be a practical choice. They reduce setup complexity and make the project easier to move, inspect, and recover.
json-persistence.full-sec-2-text: File-based persistence still needs discipline. The application must control write timing, prevent corrupted updates, and keep a clear structure for each data domain. In other words, JSON is simple, but not careless: it works best when the developer treats state layout, backup strategy, and validation as part of the architecture.
json-persistence.project-ai-userbot-case-text: JSON persistence is part of why Dekrov AI Userbot stays portable: runtime state, owner knowledge, reminders, memory layers, and control settings can survive restarts without a separate database server. That tradeoff matches the product well because it keeps inspection, backup, and deployment straightforward.
json-persistence.project-visitor-ai-case-text: For the visitor assistant, JSON persistence stores lightweight session state, moderation state, and review signals without introducing a heavy backend. It keeps the public layer simple to deploy while still letting consultations feel stateful and controlled.

idea-modal-description: Share a project, feature, automation, tool, or experiment.
idea-success-text: Thanks. Your suggestion has been recorded successfully.
idea-reason-placeholder: Optional context, expected value, or use case.
idea-error-main-length: Main idea must be 500 characters or fewer.
idea-error-reason-length: Reason must be 400 characters or fewer.
idea-error-contact-length: Contact must be 100 characters or fewer.
idea-error-send: Could not send the idea. Please try again later.
